meaning: n.暴晒,日光浴,[医]日射病,中暑
examples: Monthly mean insolation hours have been decreasing in summer in recent years.
The energy output from a monocrystalline cell varies with insolation level in an almost linear fashion across its operating range.
Special attention was given to insolation to sunlight and eyesight on the east and west elevations, both of which are front facades.
Those 11 square miles receive more solar insolation annually than any other comparably sized locale in North America.
